data.description:

Manycore Tech Inc. ( (HK:0068) ) has provided an announcement.

Manycore Tech Inc. has granted 1,130,000 restricted share units to a group of eligible employees under its post-IPO RSU plan, with no purchase price and a minimum vesting horizon of 12 months. None of the grantees are directors, chief executives, substantial shareholders, or other related parties, meaning the award does not require separate shareholder approval.

The RSUs are structured across multi-year vesting schedules extending up to 2029, with allocations tailored among different employee groups to provide staggered long-term incentives. The remuneration committee views the grant as market-competitive and designed to reward past performance, retain high-performing staff, and strengthen alignment between employees and shareholders, even though no specific performance targets are attached to the awards.

More about Manycore Tech Inc.

Manycore Tech Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the technology sector and uses equity-based incentives as part of its compensation strategy. The company focuses on aligning employee rewards with shareholder interests through post-IPO restricted share unit plans aimed at attracting and retaining skilled technical and leadership talent in a competitive market.

data.title: Standard Chartered updates market on voting rights and share capital
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Standard Chartered ( (GB:STAN) ) has provided an announcement.

As at 30 June 2026, Standard Chartered’s issued ordinary share capital stood at 2,191,045,499 shares of $0.50 each, giving rise to an equal number of voting rights, with each ordinary share carrying one vote and no shares held in treasury, clarifying the total pool of shareholder voting power.

The disclosure, made under UK Financial Conduct Authority transparency rules, provides investors and regulators with an updated denominator for calculating shareholding thresholds and notification duties, reinforcing the bank’s regulatory compliance and offering stakeholders clear visibility on its capital and governance structure.

More about Standard Chartered

Standard Chartered PLC is a London-headquartered international banking group incorporated as a public limited company in England and Wales. It operates primarily as a universal bank, offering a broad range of financial services across retail, corporate and institutional segments, with a significant presence in Asia, Africa and the Middle East, and its shares are listed in Hong Kong under stock code 02888.

The group is governed by a board chaired by Maria da Conceicao das Neves Calha Ramos, with William Thomas Winters serving as Group Chief Executive and a slate of independent non-executive directors providing oversight, underscoring the bank’s adherence to international corporate governance standards and regulatory requirements in its key markets.

As at 30 June 2026, Standard Chartered’s issued ordinary share capital stood at 2,191,045,499 shares of $0.50 each, giving rise to an equal number of voting rights, with each ordinary share carrying one vote and no shares held in treasury, clarifying the total pool of shareholder voting power.

The disclosure, made under UK Financial Conduct Authority transparency rules, provides investors and regulators with an updated denominator for calculating shareholding thresholds and notification duties, reinforcing the bank’s regulatory compliance and offering stakeholders clear visibility on its capital and governance structure.

data.title: Carry Wealth Faces Court Stop Notice on Major Share Block, Trading Resumes in Hong Kong
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Carry Wealth Holdings Limited ( (HK:0643) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Carry Wealth Holdings Limited disclosed that it has been served with a stop notice from the High Court of Hong Kong, restraining the transfer and dividend payments of 411,289,396 shares registered in the name of MARS Worldwide Holdings Limited. The notice stems from Giant Chance International Development Limited’s claim to be the beneficial owner of these shares following an alleged default under an April 2026 loan agreement and a related securities account charge.

The board, after inquiry with major beneficial owner Mr. Sun Banggui, reports that Mr. Sun disputes the allegation and may initiate legal proceedings, including seeking discharge of the stop notice. The company currently believes the order has no immediate and direct impact on its operations or financial position, but will monitor developments, seek legal advice as needed, and has cautioned shareholders and potential investors while resuming trading in its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange following a brief suspension.

More about Carry Wealth Holdings Limited

Carry Wealth Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company whose shares trade on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. Its shareholder base includes interests held through MARS Worldwide Holdings Limited and Zephyrus Capital Limited, entities controlled predominantly by investor Mr. Sun Banggui, with a minority beneficial interest held by Ms. Zhu Ruiping.

The company operates under Hong Kong’s securities and listing regulatory framework and is subject to disclosure obligations for inside information. Its board currently consists of two executive directors, Mr. Li Xiaoming and Mr. Lee Chi Ho, and two independent non-executive directors, Mr. Lam Chi Wing and Ms. Li Qian, reflecting a typical governance structure for mid-sized listed firms.

While the announcement focuses on share ownership and legal matters, management emphasizes that there is no immediate effect on business operations or financial position. This suggests the firm’s core activities and revenue streams are not directly tied to the disputed share block, although any extended dispute could still influence market perception and shareholder dynamics.

Average Trading Volume: 1,261,491

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Dynagreen Leaves Nearly Entire RMB2.36 Billion Convertible Bond Issue Unconverted
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1330) ) has shared an update.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group reported that its A share convertible corporate bonds, issued in February 2022 for RMB2.36 billion with a six-year term and listed in Shanghai under the name Dynagreen Convertible Bonds, have undergone multiple conversion price adjustments following consecutive annual and interim equity distributions and an A-share incentive scheme. These price resets, which lowered the conversion price from RMB9.82 at issuance to RMB8.67 per share as of July 2026, are designed to reflect shareholder returns and employee incentives while maintaining bondholder conversion economics.

In the second quarter of 2026, only 112 A shares were converted from the Dynagreen Convertible Bonds, worth RMB1,000, bringing total converted bonds since 2022 to RMB143,000, or just 14,798 shares, representing 0.0011% of pre-conversion share capital. As of 30 June 2026, RMB2.359857 billion of the bonds, or 99.9939% of the original issue size, remained outstanding and available for conversion until February 2028, indicating limited bondholder appetite to convert so far and leaving Dynagreen with a largely intact convertible debt overhang on its balance sheet.

More about Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated environmental services company focused on waste-to-energy and related environmental protection projects, with its A shares and convertible bonds traded in mainland China and H shares listed in Hong Kong. The group finances its operations partly through onshore A-share convertible corporate bonds issued on the Shanghai Stock Exchange, reflecting its reliance on capital markets to support long-term infrastructure and environmental initiatives.

data.description:

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Richly Field China Development Limited ( (HK:0313) ) has issued an announcement.

Richly Field China Development Limited reported a sharp decline in revenue to HK$41.9 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, down from HK$123.2 million a year earlier, with gross profit nearly halving amid continued weakness in its property operations. The group posted a net loss of HK$214.1 million, narrower than the prior year, but results were heavily hit by substantial impairments on goodwill, other receivables and investment properties, highlighting ongoing asset quality pressures and underlining the financial strain on the developer as it navigates a difficult real estate market.

The company also recorded lower selling and administrative expenses and a modest income tax credit, but financing costs remained high and profitability was further undermined by valuation losses on its investment properties. While some reversal of expected credit losses on trade receivables provided minor relief, the scale of impairments indicates continued headwinds for stakeholders, signalling persistent balance-sheet risks and limited near-term earnings visibility for the Hong Kong-listed developer.

More about Richly Field China Development Limited

Richly Field China Development Limited is a Hong Kong-listed property developer with operations conducted through its subsidiaries. The group focuses on investment properties and related real estate activities in mainland China, deriving revenue from property sales and associated services in a challenging market environment.

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6938) ) has shared an update.

Suzhou Ribo Life Science has reached the first candidate drug nomination milestone in its siRNA collaboration with Madrigal Pharmaceuticals, advancing an RNA therapeutic for liver diseases with an initial focus on metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis. The achievement triggers the start of IND-enabling studies, positioning the partners to move toward clinical development in a large, underserved global MASH population, while the company cautions that there is no guarantee the collaboration will ultimately lead to completed transactions or approved products.

The milestone underscores Suzhou Ribo’s strategy to apply its validated RiboGalSTAR liver-targeting platform to high-burden liver indications where treatment needs remain significant. Successful progress in this program could strengthen the company’s standing in the RNA therapeutics field and potentially create long-term value for shareholders and patients if subsequent development stages are achieved.

More about Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company specializing in RNA therapeutics, particularly small interfering RNA, with a focus on liver-targeted drug delivery via its proprietary RiboGalSTAR platform. The company develops innovative treatments for serious liver diseases and partners with global pharma players to advance its pipeline.

data.title: Insilico Strikes Up to USD600 Million AI Drug Discovery Pact With Takeda
data.description:

An update from InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo ( (HK:3696) ) is now available.

InSilico Medicine has entered a strategic collaboration with Japanese biopharmaceutical group Takeda to deploy its end-to-end AI drug discovery platform, Pharma.AI, to identify novel, clinically differentiated drug candidates across Takeda’s core therapeutic areas. Under the agreement, Insilico will lead AI-driven molecular discovery, while Takeda will take selected candidates through global clinical development, gaining exclusive worldwide rights to develop, manufacture, and commercialize any resulting therapeutics.

The deal provides Insilico with about USD60 million in upfront and near-term payments and could reach a total value of up to USD600 million through success-based milestones, plus tiered royalties on future product sales. The partnership underscores growing pharma demand for AI-enabled R&D, strengthens Insilico’s positioning as a key AI partner to large drugmakers, and signals potential long-term upside for the company’s revenue streams and shareholders if the collaboration yields commercially successful medicines.

More about InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is a Hong Kong–listed global biotechnology company that integrates artificial intelligence and automation to accelerate drug discovery and innovation in life sciences. Through its proprietary Pharma.AI platform and in-house R&D capabilities, it develops drug candidates targeting unmet medical needs in areas such as fibrosis, oncology, immunology, pain, and obesity and metabolic disorders, while also extending its AI technologies to advanced materials, agriculture, nutritional products, and veterinary medicine.

data.description:

  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited ( (HK:0474) ) has provided an announcement.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited has announced it will miss the 30 June 2026 deadline to publish its audited annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026, citing the need for additional time to collect valuation data for certain financial assets, associates and investment property required by its auditor. The board has decided not to issue unaudited management accounts to avoid confusing investors, and stresses that group operations remain normal while it works with the auditor to complete remaining procedures and release the results as soon as practicable.

Because of the delay in audited results, the company expects it may also miss the 31 July 2026 deadline to dispatch its 2026 annual report, which would put it in non-compliance with listing rules until a new date is announced. The board meeting originally scheduled for 30 June 2026 to approve the results has been postponed, and trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 2 July 2026 pending publication of the overdue annual results, a move that directly affects shareholder liquidity and market perception of the group’s governance and reporting reliability.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the life sciences sector through a group structure incorporated in the Cayman Islands. data.title: Hao Tian International Delays 2026 Results, Faces Trading Suspension in Hong Kong
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An update from Hao Tian International Construction Investment Group Limited ( (HK:1341) ) is now available.

Hao Tian International Construction Investment Group Limited has announced it will not be able to publish its audited annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026 by the 30 June regulatory deadline, citing the need for additional time to complete valuations of certain financial assets, associates and prepayments required for the audit. The board has decided against releasing unaudited management accounts to avoid potentially confusing investors, and while it stresses that the group’s operations remain normal, it cannot provide a firm timetable for completion and will update shareholders in due course.

Because of the delay in finalising the 2026 results, the company expects a knock-on delay in sending out its 2026 annual report, which would put it in non-compliance with Hong Kong listing rules governing timely reporting. The board meeting originally scheduled for 30 June 2026 to approve the results has been postponed, and trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 2 July 2026 pending publication of the overdue annual results, heightening uncertainty for investors until audited figures are available.

Sterling Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1825) ) just unveiled an update.

Sterling Group Holdings has announced that trading in its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 9:00 a.m. on 2 July 2026. The suspension follows a delay in publishing the audited annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026, with trading halted pending release of these 2026 financial figures.

The move highlights ongoing disclosure and reporting challenges at the company and temporarily restricts liquidity for shareholders. Investors will have limited price discovery until the results are published, and the suspension may raise governance and transparency concerns in the market regarding the Group’s financial reporting timetable.

More about Sterling Group Holdings Limited

Sterling Group Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The announcement does not specify its industry, products, or services, but confirms its current leadership structure, with Wong Mei Wai Alice serving as chairperson, executive director, and chief executive officer, supported by two independent non-executive directors.

Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3388) ) has issued an update.

Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. reported that its annual general meeting held on June 30, 2026, in Hong Kong was attended by most board members and the controlling shareholders, who together represent about 67.49% of the company’s issued H share capital. All attending shareholders, whether present in person or via electronic means, were counted in the official poll, underscoring strong participation in the company’s governance.

Shareholders approved all ordinary resolutions, including the 2025 board work report, the independent non-executive directors’ duty performance report, and the 2025 profit distribution plan, reflecting broad support for management’s past performance and capital allocation. They also backed the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor for 2026 and granted the board general mandates to repurchase and issue H shares, enhancing the company’s financial flexibility and potential tools for future capital management and strategic financing.

More about Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-incorporated joint stock company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the technology and manufacturing sector with a focus on 3D printing-related products and services. Its shareholder base is dominated by several controlling shareholders and related entities, which collectively hold a majority of the company’s issued H shares and exert significant influence over corporate decisions.
